target: 11. Fans Rushing In
July 24
Shinpei Ajiro
Mio, two A Lunches. Take them out.
Got it! Next, four C Lunches please!
O-Oh.
Thank you very much!
(It's a full-blown rush today with no holding back...)
*Yawn*...
Hey, Shin-chan. Don't yawn so widely inside the cafe.
What if the customers see you?
Ugh... sorry.
Did you not sleep much? I told you to go to bed early.
I really wanted to, but we don't know when Mio's shadow might show up...
Could it be... you stayed up because of that?
Pretty much.
...You'll ruin your health. Don't push yourself too hard, okay?
I'll make sure to be careful too.
Four C Lunches, ready! Take them out.
Coming!
Aren't there a lot of tourists today?
Yeah... this might be the first time it's ever been this crowded.
Tourists usually come around the time of the summer festival, but it feels like there's an exceptionally large number this year.
Man~~ it's so hot~~.
--Wait, what the heck!? It's super crowded!
Tetsu-san, welcome. The counter is open.
Thanks, Mio-chan. I'll have the usual...
Daaad~ One B Lunch, add fried shrimp, and a medium serving of rice.
Haaah~~~~...
You look incredibly tired.
It's been a nightmare today! Tourists have been pouring in non-stop since this morning!
Every year around this time, me and the folks at the town hall prepare for the increase in tourists, but...
This year is nothing compared to normal years! Jeez, it's so busy...!
Totsumura-san, that's probably...
Yeah, I already have a pretty good idea. The true identity of these extra tourists is, nine times out of ten...
Kaori-chan's fans!
I figured...
Just like Karikiri-san said.
--Hm, an email?
Could it be from Kaori-chan?
Yeah, seems like it. Let's see...
What did it say?
Today's rehearsal has been canceled.
Eh... really?
A huge crowd of fans rushed over, so apparently she can't easily go outside.
So this time, it's straight to the actual performance without a rehearsal. I wonder if it'll be okay...
(Judging from this email... Koyuba-san is probably feeling really down.)
The crowd of customers is thinning out too... Mio, I'm gonna go give Koyuba-san a quick call.
Ah, if you're doing that, tell Kaori-chan that I'm cheering for her.
Yeah, got it.
Hello, Koyuba-san?
Ah... Shinpei-kun?
I saw your email. Is today's live show going to be okay?
Thanks for worrying about me. Actually, things have gotten a bit troublesome...
Did something happen?
It's not just that the secret live show's venue is Hitogashima, but even the inn I'm staying at...
It seems they even figured out my room number...
Eh!?
Right now, thanks to Karikiri-san's kindness, I'm staying at the shrine office of Hitogashima Shrine. I'm planning to stay put here until the live show.
I see...
"Since it's a shrine, it's safe."
"Security items are important."
Since it's a shrine, it's safe. Plus you can move to the live stage right away.
Yeah. Karikiri-san has been talking to me about all sorts of things, so it's been fun.
Ah... Doesn't that guy talk for way too long?
Hehehe... maybe so. But he's an interesting person, Karikiri-san.
It seems he usually doesn't have anyone he can talk to about Tokyo with, so we ended up having a pretty lively conversation.
I see... Well, as long as you're enjoying yourself, that's what matters.
But if the conversation drags on too long, feel free to cut it short, okay? He's not the type to get angry over that.
Hehe, got it. If it gets tough, I'll do that.
Security items are important. Since it's a shrine, I think it'll probably be fine, but it might be best to at least have them ready.
Yeah, you don't need to worry about that.
Actually, due to my manager's policy, I always make sure to carry security items on me during live shows anyway.
Oh, is that so? Then that's reassuring.
Hehe, somehow Shinpei-kun, you're sounding a lot like my manager.
But... I'm sorry about today. I really wanted to let you and Mio-chan watch the rehearsal.
No, it's not your fault, Koyuba-san.
But you were looking forward to it...
It's okay. Mio and I still haven't changed our minds about wanting to help you.
Eh...
No matter what happens, we're cheering for you, and we're definitely going to watch the live show. So please, absolutely do not give up.
No matter how many unexpected things happen, make the live show you aimed for a reality on this island.
Yeah, you're right... That's exactly why I came back.
I... I'll see it through. Watch me, Shinpei-kun.
Yeah, I'm looking forward to it. Anyway, since you suddenly have some free time, you should take it easy until the actual performance.
Yeah... I will.
Thank you for calling... I'm really glad I got to hear your voice. Well then, see you later!
Yeah, see you later.
(To think they'd even manage to pinpoint her room right away...)
(But an increase in tourists isn't entirely a bad thing.)
(The more people there are on the island, the harder it becomes for the Shadows to avoid people's eyes, which will end up restricting their movements.)
(Though I still can't let my guard down...)
(I hope Koyuba-san's live show is a success, and we can all make some great summer festival memories together.)
